title: Plastics Strategy - Environment - European Commission
body: Overview Plastics are an important material in our economy and daily lives. However, they can have serious negative effects on the environment and human health. The EU adopted a European Strategy for Plastics in a Circular Economy in January 2018, as part of the EU’s First Circular Economy Action Plan , which builds on existing measures to reduce plastic waste. The strategy is a key element of Europe’s transition towards a carbon-neutral and circular economy. It will contribute to reaching the 2030 Sustainable Development Goals, the Paris Climate Agreement objectives and the EU’s industrial policy objectives. © Sami Sert/AlexKazachok2 | iStockphoto Objectives The Plastics Strategy aims to: Protect our environment and reduce marine litter, greenhouse gas emissions and our dependence on imported fossil fuels , thus supporting more sustainable and safer consumption and production patterns for plastics. The strategy also aims to transform the way plastic products are designed, produced, used and recycled in the EU. Actions Making recycling profitable for business , with new rules on packaging to improve the recyclability of plastics and increase the demand for recycled plastic content. Curbing plastic waste , including through the Directive on Single-Use Plastic Products , measures to restrict the use and proliferation of microplastics. Driving innovation and investment by scaling up support for innovation, with an additional €100 million to develop smarter and more recyclable plastic materials and making recycling processes more efficient. Spurring global change by working with our international partners to devise global solutions and develop international standards on plastics, including through the once-in-a-generation Global Plastics Treaty. For a full list of actions and their timelines, see Annex I of the Plastics Strategy . See our chemicals , circular economy , plastics , and waste and recycling pages for related policies. Timeline Previous and upcoming actions 12 August 2026 Packaging and Packaging Waste Regulation (PPWR) enters into application 11 February 2025 PPWR enters into force 19 December 2024 European Council adopts PPWR, replacing 1994 Directive Sep-Oct 2023 Adoption of several initiatives on microplastics REACH restriction addressing intentionally added microplastics Proposal for a Regulation on preventing pellet losses to reduce microplastics pollution Brochure on EU action against microplastic pollution 30 November 2022 European Commission adopts Communication on policy framework for biobased, biodegradable and compostable plastics Find out more 11 March 2020 European Commission adopts new Circular Economy Action Plan, including revised legislative proposals on waste 2 July 2019 Directive on Single-Use Plastics enters into force 28 May 2018 Commission adopts proposal for Directive on Single-Use Plastics Show 5 more items 16 January 2018 Commission adopts EU Plastics Strategy Related links Connected strategies Biodiversity Strategy to 2030 Circular Economy Action Plan Chemicals Strategy for Sustainability European Green Deal European Industrial Strategy Zero Pollution Action Plan Documents and publications Commission Communication – Plastics strategy Staff Working Document – Plastics strategy Annexes – Plastics strategy Brochure - This data can aid plastics regulation, but more transparency is still needed. 5 min read Press release 23 December 2025 New package of measures to boost circular economy and strengthen Europe's plastic recycling The European Commission has unveiled a first set of pilot actions to accelerate Europe's transition to a circular economy, with a particular focus on the plastics sector. 1 min read See all news on plastics Share this page
